compositional_truth_check.details: The biome has suffered a catastrophic loss of biological specimens. P1 (String of Nickels), P2 (Mexican Mint), and P3 (Pothos) are no longer visible or have completely desiccated/decomposed. The white rabbit anchor is missing from the frame in the final two images.

plant_audit.P1_P2_P4_Cluster: The yellow pot (P1) and black pots (P2/P4) are devoid of viable plant matter. Only soil and scattered debris remain.
plant_audit.P3_Anchor: The white rabbit figurine, previously serving as the scale anchor, has been removed or displaced from the field of view.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Appears dry and compacted. No signs of active growth or moisture.
biome_observations.debris: Presence of crushed eggshells (calcium amendment or waste) remains in the black pot, but no plant matter is associated with it.
biome_observations.surface_anomalies: The desk surface is dark and lacks the previous botanical activity.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_T-2: Gradual loss of leaf turgor and eventual disappearance of the primary Pothos leaf.
temporal_deltas.T-1_to_current: Total disappearance of the scale anchor (rabbit) and complete cessation of visible plant life.

visual_health_inference.conclusion: The biome is currently non-functional. All registered plants have either been removed or have perished due to environmental stress or neglect.
visual_health_inference.methodology: Visual inspection confirms zero chlorophyll-bearing tissue remaining in the pots.

anomalies: Removal of the white rabbit scale anchor.
Total absence of the registered botanical specimens.
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit reveals a complete collapse of the indoor botanical setup. Starting from the earliest image, where a single Pothos leaf and a rabbit anchor were present, the sequence documents a rapid decline. By the final image, the pots are empty, the anchor is gone, and the biome is effectively defunct. The presence of eggshells suggests an attempt at soil enrichment that failed to sustain the specimens.
